npp-plugins.description:
Archive manager for Nextpad++, powered by the in-process 7-Zip engine.

Features:
- Dockable two-pane panel: filesystem browser on top, archive contents below
- Opens 7z, zip, tar, gz, bz2, xz, RAR / RAR5 and more (RAR is extraction-only)
- Transparent nested-archive descent (e.g. site.tar.gz)
- Open a file from inside an archive in the editor; saving writes it back into the archive
- Create archives with a full Add-to-Archive dialog (format, level, method, dictionary, solid, threads, encryption)
- Extract with path mode, overwrite mode and eliminate-root options
- Password-protected archives: prompts for a password (with Show Password) when extracting or opening
- Test archives and compute CRC32 / MD5 / SHA-1 / SHA-256 / SHA-384 / SHA-512 checksums

Universal binary (Apple Silicon + Intel). Bundles the 7-Zip 7z.so engine.

Engine: 7-Zip by Igor Pavlov (LGPL + unRAR restriction). Plugin: GPL.
macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus-plugins/NextZip.macos
npp-plugins.author: Andrey Letov

npp-plugins.description:
FTP, FTPS, FTPES and SFTP client for Nextpad++.

Features:
- Dockable panel with connection profiles, a remote file tree, and a transfer queue
- Download, upload, rename, delete, set permissions, create files/directories
- Download && open: fetch a remote file and open it in the editor
- Edit on server: saving a file you opened from the server auto-uploads it back
- Download a whole folder recursively to a chosen local folder
- Copy / Cut / Paste files and folders on the server
- Separate "NppFTP - Output" log panel

Universal binary (Apple Silicon + Intel).

macOS port of Harry's NppFTP plugin for Notepad++.
macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus-plugins/NppFTP.macos
npp-plugins.author: Harry

npp-plugins.description:
URL Encoder and Decoder plug-in for Notepad++ which helps to encode/decode URLs within Notepad++.

How it works:
1. Configure settings from menu (Plugins > URL Plugin > URL Plugin Settings).
2. Select text/URL which you want to encode/decode.
3. Perform your task via Plugins > URL Plugin > Encode/Decode URL.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/nppURLPlugin
npp-plugins.author: Rajendra Singh

npp-plugins.description:
Compare two files side by side with full diff highlighting, alignment, and navigation.

Features:
- File and selection comparison with Myers diff algorithm
- Changed/added/removed/moved line detection with color highlighting
- Character-level diff highlighting within changed lines
- Diff block navigation (Next/Previous/First/Last)
- Vertical scroll synchronization
- Compare summary statistics

Coming soon:
- Find Unique Lines
- Diff since last Save
- Compare file/selection to Clipboard
- SVN Diff / Git Diff
- Auto Re-Compare on Change

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/ComparePlus
npp-plugins.author: Pavel Nedev

npp-plugins.description:
Doxygen comment generator for Notepad++. Put your cursor on the blank line above a function, press Ctrl+Alt+Shift+D, and NppDoxy inserts a /** ... */ block with @brief, one @param line per argument, @return, and @details — each pre-filled with $(...) jump markers. Tab cycles through the markers; Escape clears them.

Features:
- Comment Function — parse the function signature below the cursor and generate a fully-populated doc block
- Comment File — insert a file-header block with @file + @brief
- Active Commenting — type /** above a function and press Enter, and NppDoxy auto-runs Comment Function
- Jump locations — Tab through $(placeholder) markers in the generated block; Escape clears them
- Language-specific doc_start/doc_line/doc_end delimiters

Supported languages: C, C++, Java, Python, PHP, JavaScript, C#.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/NppDoxy
npp-plugins.author: Andrey Letov

npp-plugins.description:
IndentByFold uses Scintilla fold points to indent code. Language-independent — works with any language that has fold points.

Features:
- Reindent File command (entire document)
- Auto-indent on Enter based on fold levels
- Auto-dedent on closing constructs (e.g. })
- Language-aware fallback paths for Ruby, HTML, Lisp, Lua, Pascal, XML
- Skips Python reindent (Python uses significant whitespace)

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/indentbyfold
npp-plugins.author: Ben Bluemel, Frank Fesevur

npp-plugins.description:
Reverses the order of selected lines or all lines in the document.

macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/qkNppReverseLines

npp-plugins.description:

macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/FoldingLineHider
npp-plugins.author: Leonard Chai

npp-plugins.description:
Pipe selected text through user-defined external programs. Supports macros, input/output files, async execution. Includes 7 built-in commands (sort, compress, base64) and a Java demo.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/pork2sausage
npp-plugins.author: Don HO

npp-plugins.description:
Opens the system hosts file (/etc/hosts) in a new Notepad++ tab with a single menu click. Adds a "Plugins > Open Host file > Open" menu item. Handy for quickly inspecting or editing host overrides.

Note: /etc/hosts is root-owned on macOS, so saving edits requires elevating privileges separately.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/NppPluginOpenHost
npp-plugins.author: jejemorg

npp-plugins.description:
Auto-copies the current selection to the system clipboard whenever it changes. Enable via Plugins > Selection to Clipboard > Auto copy selection to clipboard.

Heads up: macOS has only one clipboard, so enabling this feature overwrites your Cmd+C clipboard content every time the selection changes. The default is off. Intended for users migrating from PuTTY / xterm workflows who expect drag-select to copy.

Features:
- Automatic copy of stream and rectangular (column) selections
- Preserves embedded blank lines in the copied block
- Per-tab selection-change tracking
- Toggle state persisted across restarts (saved on every toggle, survives force-quit)

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/SelectToClipboard
npp-plugins.author: Jakub Dvorak

npp-plugins.description:
Export the current document (or a selection) to RTF or HTML with full syntax highlighting preserved — fonts, colors, bold/italic/underline per Scintilla style. Five menu items:

- Export to RTF (save to file)
- Export to HTML (save to file)
- Copy RTF to clipboard
- Copy HTML to clipboard
- Copy all formats to clipboard

Clipboard commands put the rich flavor (public.rtf / public.html) for rich paste targets (Mail, Word, TextEdit Rich) and the rendered source markup as the plain-text fallback so pasting into a plain-text target (another Notepad++ tab, Terminal) shows the same bytes the file export would write. "Copy all" puts both rich flavors plus a concatenated HTML + RTF + plain text blob.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/NPP_ExportPlugin
npp-plugins.author: Christian Grasser

npp-plugins.description:
Six favorite-file slots with keyboard shortcuts. Store up to six frequently-opened files and jump to any of them with Ctrl+Opt+1..6. Ctrl+Opt+B opens the JSON config file for editing, and the config is reloaded on every open so edits take effect immediately.

Config is stored at ~/.notepad++/plugins/Config/NppFavorites.json. First run seeds /etc/hosts and /etc/shells as sample favorites — edit the JSON to point at whatever files you want.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/nppfavorites
npp-plugins.author: Helder Sepulveda

npp-plugins.description:
Auto-detects whether the current buffer uses tabs or N-space indentation and configures Scintilla so the Tab key inserts the file's actual indent style. No configuration needed — just install, open files, and Tab always does the right thing.

Scans up to 5,000 lines per buffer on tab activation, skips ' * ' continuation lines inside /** */ doc blocks (C/C++/C#/Obj-C/Java/PHP/JavaScript), and picks the dominant style by 4:1 majority. Results are cached per file path so switching tabs is instant.

Menu under Plugins → Auto Detect Indention: Disable plugin (toggle, persisted across restarts), Version, Goto website. Disabled-state restores whatever indent settings the host had when Notepad++ launched.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/nppAutoDetectIndent
npp-plugins.author: Mike Tzou (Chocobo1)

npp-plugins.description:
Select the text inside quotes with a single keystroke (Option+'). Uses Scintilla lexer styles to detect string boundaries in 35+ languages — correctly handles escaped quotes, raw strings, heredocs, and other language-specific string forms. For plain text and unmapped languages, a character-level quote-pair fallback scans the current line for matched quotes containing the cursor. Falls back to selecting the current word when not inside any quotes.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/selectquotedtext
npp-plugins.author: Frank Fesevur

npp-plugins.description:
Shows the file you're editing in your Discord profile status — file name, language with icon, file size, line/column, and elapsed time. Supports 30+ language-specific icons, customizable format strings with template variables, idle detection with configurable timeout, and an in-app Options dialog. Requires the Discord desktop app to be running.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/notepadpp_rpc
npp-plugins.author: Zukaritasu

npp-plugins.description:
Code snippet expansion engine. Type a trigger word, invoke Replace Snip, and the word expands to a full template with hotspot navigation. Snippets are stored per-language in QuickText.ini keyed by LangType number (3=C++, 22=Python, 58=JavaScript, 255=GLOBAL). Supports Scintilla autocomplete integration, tab-to-spaces conversion, and EOL-aware line breaks.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/nppQuickText
npp-plugins.author: Joao Moreno, TonyM, MVincent

npp-plugins.description:
Real-time Markdown preview panel. Open a .md file and toggle the panel to see a live-rendered preview alongside the editor. Supports GitHub-flavored Markdown, syntax-highlighted code blocks, relative images, dark mode, scroll synchronization, YAML frontmatter, Mermaid diagrams, and export to HTML. Rendering powered by WKWebView with marked.js + highlight.js.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/NppMarkdownPanel
npp-plugins.author: Jens Wollgarten

npp-plugins.description:
Elastic Tabstops — tab-separated text aligns into columns because adjacent lines auto-stretch to match the widest item in each column. No spaces are inserted; only the visual rendering of \t characters changes.

Features:
- Global mode (uniform column widths across the whole file) and local mode (block-local alignment)
- Whole-file or selection-only scope
- Convert Elastic Tabs <-> Spaces with pixel-faithful width preservation
- Per-extension auto-enable via JSON config
- Per-file "Remember enabled files" persistence across restarts
- Split-view aware (independent state for primary/secondary view)
- Large-range warning (>1000 lines) before Space->ET conversion

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/ElasticTabstops
npp-plugins.author: Justin Dailey, Marius Vasiliu

npp-plugins.description:
Tree-view navigator for XML documents. Opens a floating panel that renders the active XML document as a hierarchical outline; click any node to jump the editor caret to that position.

Features:
- Compact +/- disclosure markers (no native triangles) matching the Search Results Panel footprint
- Dense row packing with Cmd+=, Cmd+-, Cmd+0 to zoom the tree font (default 11pt)
- Hand-rolled UTF-8 XML tokenizer tolerant of ill-formed XML with precise byte offsets per node
- Filter text field with all-tokens-must-match semantics (order-independent, case-insensitive)
- Attribute values rendered inline as 'element [attrValue1] [attrValue2]'
- Six-item right-click menu: Go to Node Start/End, Go to Content Start/End, Select Node/Content
- Auto-reload on buffer activation, file save, and text changes

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/XmlNavigator
npp-plugins.author: Christian Grasser

npp-plugins.description:
Tree-view navigator, formatter, compressor and validator for JSON documents. Docks into the side-panel host (with pop-out to a floating utility window); click any node to jump the editor caret to its line/column.

Features:
- RapidJSON SAX parser with TrackingStream for line/column per token
- Format / Compress / Sort-by-key with configurable indent (tab / spaces / auto), EOL (CRLF / LF / CR / auto), and single-line-arrays toggle
- Validate shows the offending position highlighted in the editor
- Right-click menu: Copy / Copy name / Copy value / Copy path / Expand all / Collapse all
- Live tree-filter search with auto-expand
- Live re-parse on buffer edits (debounced)
- Lazy NSOutlineView data source — sub-300ms render for multi-MB JSON files
- Cmd+Option+Shift J/M/C/K shortcuts matching the Windows Ctrl+Alt+Shift chord
- Follow-current-tab / auto-format-on-open / ignore-comments-and-trailing-commas / replace-undefined settings

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/JSON-Viewer
npp-plugins.author: NPP-JSONViewer team

npp-plugins.description:
Send selected text to any LLM backend and stream the reply back into the editor. Shortcut: Cmd+Shift+O.

Supported backends:
- OpenAI / Azure OpenAI
- Anthropic Claude (/v1/messages)
- Ollama (native /api/generate)
- LM Studio, vLLM, LocalAI, LiteLLM (OpenAI-compatible)
- AnythingLLM, Groq, OpenRouter
- Any other OpenAI-compatible endpoint

Features:
- Streaming reply with main-thread-safe chunk delivery and cancel
- Wire-compatible NppLLM.ini schema with the Windows NppOpenAI plugin (auto-migrates NppOpenAI.ini on first load)
- Multi-prompt support via [Prompt:Name] sections in an instructions file (picker sheet at request time)
- <think>…</think> filtering for reasoning models (toggle via show_reasoning)
- Keep-my-question toggle (replace vs. append)
- Chat mode with configurable history window
- Default INI ships commented-out [API] blocks for 9 backends — just copy one into the live section
- Dark-mode-aware sheets

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/NppLLM
npp-plugins.author: Richard Stockinger

npp-plugins.description:
Dockable AI-chat side panel for Notepad++. Talk to OpenAI, Google Gemini, Anthropic Claude — or any OpenAI-compatible local server (LM Studio, AnythingLLM, Ollama-via-shim, LiteLLM, vLLM, LocalAI) via a per-provider endpoint URL override. Shortcut: Cmd+Shift+A.

Features:
- Dock panel with chat history + input, word-wrap, Cmd+/-/0 to zoom
- Right-click selection actions: Explain (panel only), Refactor / Add Comments / Fix (replace selection)
- Scenario-tuned prompt composer: 5 scenario flags × 3 output rules × 3 detail levels × 5 encodings, live-preview in Settings
- Per-provider Default Model that persists across launches
- Per-provider Endpoint URL override (Gemini supports {model} placeholder) so any provider row can hit a local or self-hosted server
- API keys stored in the macOS Keychain (service: NppAIAssistant), never on disk
- Smart-substitution defence on credential fields + normalization on save (pasted keys with en-dashes or curly quotes get cleaned automatically)
- Dark-mode-aware, theme-adaptive text colors
- Universal arm64+x86_64 binary, macOS 11+

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/NppAIAssistant
npp-plugins.author: PingKuei Lin

npp-plugins.description:
Docked side panel that puts the Beads issue tracker (https://github.com/gastownhall/beads) inside Notepad++. Six surfaces — Dashboard, Issues list, Insights (PageRank / Betweenness / Critical Path / k-core / articulation points / cycles + Project Velocity + Graph Health), force-directed Graph (Heatmap, Critical Path, What-If overlays; Execution-Flow ↔ Dependency-Flow arrow toggle; seven layout presets), Kanban Board with Raw / Effective status modes and drag-to-update, Activity feed with the "● N new" status-bar badge.

Editor integration: bead-id detection (bd-XXX pattern) in any open buffer with three commands — Jump to bead under caret (Cmd-Opt-Shift-J), Copy bead id, Create issue from selection (Cmd-Opt-Shift-N).

Live sync via 750ms file watcher on .beads/issues.jsonl + 2s bd list poll. Federation, Jira/GitHub/Linear/Asana bridges, and all data ops go through the bd CLI; install bd separately. Universal arm64+x86_64.

A standalone Beads.app shell (no Notepad++ required) is built from the same source tree; download the DMG from the same GitHub release.

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/NppBeads
npp-plugins.author: Andrey Letov

npp-plugins.description:
Native hex editor for Nextpad++. Directly edits any open buffer as a grid of bytes: overwrite + append in hex and ASCII panes, bit-precise editing in binary notation, rectangular (block) selection with Option-drag, multi-GB copy / paste with O(window) RAM use via lazy pasteboard promises, find + find-and-replace (auto-detects ASCII vs hex patterns), go-to-offset (decimal / hex / relative), 8 / 16 / 32 / 64-bit grouping, big- / little-endian display, configurable address width + columns, compare-against-file with red-highlighted diffs, cross-app paste that strips address columns and ASCII glosses from lldb / gdb / xxd / x64dbg / IDA / C-array dumps.

Original Windows author: Jens Lorenz (HexEditor v0.9.x, https://sourceforge.net/projects/npp-plugins/files/Hex%20Editor/). Windows GitHub maintainer: Christian Grasser (https://github.com/chcg/NPP_HexEdit). macOS port (fresh native rewrite preserving Windows-faithful UX and clipboard semantics): Dave Hadner.

macOS Port Author: Dave Hadner
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/dhadner/NPP_HexEdit

npp-plugins.description:
Remove or replace "gremlin" characters — invisible, control, or non-ASCII characters that sneak into files and cause subtle bugs (BBEdit-style "Zap Gremlins").

Search categories:
- Non-ASCII characters (above U+007F)
- Control characters (C0 except Tab/CR/LF, plus DEL)
- Null (ASCII 0) characters

Actions:
- Delete
- Replace with a character of your choice
- Replace with a \uXXXX code, optionally transliterating common typographic characters to ASCII first (smart quotes, dashes, NBSP, ...)
- Replace with an HTML entity (named where available, else numeric)

Operates on the selection if any, else the whole document, as one undo step. Includes an optional "Highlight gremlins" toggle.

macOS Author: Andrey Letov
Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus/NppZapGremlins
npp-plugins.author: Andrey Letov

npp-plugins.description:
Live spell-checking with a bundled, self-contained Hunspell engine.

Features:
- Red-squiggle underlining of misspelled words as you type/scroll
- Left single-click a misspelled word for suggestions (Ignore / Add to dictionary)
- Find next/previous misspelling; copy/erase/bookmark misspellings
- Multiple simultaneous languages; change current language
- In code, check only comments/strings/identifiers (per-lexer aware)
- File-type filtering; debug log
- In-app Download Dictionaries dialog (LibreOffice/OpenOffice .aff/.dic)
- Settings (Simple + Advanced)

Dictionaries are downloaded on demand (Change Current Language > Download Languages).

macOS Author: Andrey Letov

npp-plugins.description:
Manage multiple named sessions of open files.

Sessions are stored as Apple property lists in the plugin's configuration folder.

npp-plugins.description:
Format and beautify T-SQL with configurable indentation, keyword casing, and line breaks.

Features:
- Format the current selection, or the whole document when nothing is selected
- Settings dialog: indent string (\t / \s), spaces per tab, max line width, statement/clause breaks
- Toggles: expand comma lists, trailing commas, space after expanded comma, expand boolean expressions, expand CASE statements, expand BETWEEN conditions, expand IN lists, uppercase keywords, break JOIN ON sections, standardize keywords
- Keyword standardization (e.g. PROC → PROCEDURE, JOIN → INNER JOIN)
- [noformat] / [minify] inline regions, and a parse-error "continue?" warning
- Settings persist in the plugins config directory

The formatting engine is a faithful C++ reimplementation of the original .NET library, validated byte-for-byte against the upstream Windows test corpus.

Universal binary (Apple Silicon + Intel).

Original plugin by Justin Dailey (dail8859) (GPL-2.0).

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus-plugins/SurroundSelection.macos
npp-plugins.author: Justin Dailey (dail8859); macOS port by Andrey Letov

npp-plugins.description:
Convert the selected text to a file and launch it on the fly — define the file type by extension and let the OS open it.

Features:
- Send the selection to a temp file and open it with its associated app
- User-defined launch commands, each with its own extension
- Optional Base64 decode of the selection before launching
- Settings dialog and editable user-command list

Universal binary (Apple Silicon + Intel).

Original plugin by Don Ho (GPL v2).

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus-plugins/selectNLaunch.macos
npp-plugins.author: Don Ho; macOS port by Andrey Letov

npp-plugins.description:
Encrypt or decrypt the current document or selection in place, using a key of your choice (Blowfish cipher).

Features:
- Encrypt / decrypt the whole document
- Encrypt / decrypt the current selection
- Blowfish cipher, byte-compatible with the Windows plugin
- Password (key) dialog with confirmation

Universal binary (Apple Silicon + Intel).

Original plugin by Dominic Tobias (MIT).

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus-plugins/SecurePad.macos
npp-plugins.author: Dominic Tobias; macOS port by Andrey Letov

npp-plugins.description:
A live Python calculator inside the editor. Press Enter to evaluate the line you just finished; the result is printed on the next line. Variables and imports persist across lines.

Features:
- Evaluate the current line on Enter (toggle with "Enabled")
- "Evaluate selection" runs the selected block
- Persistent interpreter — state carries across lines
- Embedded CPython, loaded on demand from your system Python (requires python3)

Universal binary (Apple Silicon + Intel).

Original Windows plugin: pycalc (Apache-2.0).

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus-plugins/pycalc.macos
npp-plugins.author: pycalc project; macOS port by Andrey Letov

npp-plugins.description:
Compares two lists in the current document and appends a report — COMMON, LIST 1 ONLY, LIST 2 ONLY — to the end of the document.

Features:
- Two lists (one item per line) separated by a blank line
- Case-insensitive matching with per-list occurrence counts
- Report appended at the end of the document

Universal binary (Apple Silicon + Intel).

Original Windows plugin by Pablo Lopez (GPL v2).

macOS Port Author: Andrey Letov
macOS Homepage: https://github.com/nextpad-plus-plus-plugins/NppCrossCheck.macos
npp-plugins.author: Pablo Lopez; macOS port by Andrey Letov
